assessmentName: Azure Cache for Redis Production Optimization

principles: Discover Redis properties, metrics, SKU capabilities, networking, persistence, clustering, cost, and prices dynamically before applying rules.
Use Azure Cost Management actual cost as the primary current-cost signal.
Use Azure Retail Prices API for target SKU, cache size, Enterprise tier, persistence, and region price comparison.
Evaluate Redis at cache, shard, primary/replica, network, connection, memory, and hit-rate levels when metric dimensions are available.

Prefer the best valid production target: right-size SKU, scale up, scale out with clustering, tune memory policy, fix low hit rate, reduce connection churn, enable TLS/private access, or improve HA/DR.
Never recommend a cheaper target that violates memory headroom, latency, connection, throughput, eviction, persistence, clustering, availability, TLS, private access, or data-loss requirements.

lowCallCollectionPlan.goal: Collect Redis properties, metrics, cost, price, networking, and security data with the fewest REST calls while preserving recommendation quality.
callReductionPrinciples: Use subscription or resource-group list APIs instead of one GET per cache.
Cache metric definitions by resource type, location, namespace, and API version.
Fetch multiple Redis metric names in one Azure Monitor metrics request using comma-separated metricnames.
Use ShardId, Port, Primary, and Ssl dimensions to detect hot shards, non-TLS traffic, and primary/replica skew before deeper calls.
Use Cost Management grouped by ResourceId once per scope instead of querying cost per cache.
Use Retail Prices once per region and Redis meter group, cache paged results, and reuse for all caches in that region.
Fetch firewall rules, linked servers, private endpoints, diagnostic settings, or Redis configuration only when a recommendation needs that context.

derivedMetrics.shardMemorySkewRatio: max(allusedmemorypercentage by ShardId) / max(avg(allusedmemorypercentage by ShardId), 1)
derivedMetrics.shardOpsSkewRatio: max(alloperationsPerSecond by ShardId) / max(avg(alloperationsPerSecond by ShardId), 1)

requiredProductionFilters: target SKU must satisfy p99 memory plus production buffer
target SKU must satisfy p99 server load, CPU, throughput, bandwidth, and connection demand
target tier must support required clustering, persistence, replicas, zones, private endpoints, firewall rules, and TLS policy
target memory policy must match application semantics and data-loss tolerance
target scaling recommendation must consider shard skew and key distribution
target security recommendation must not break approved client connectivity paths

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.p95UsedMemoryPct') lt 40 and get_value(resource, 'signals.p95ServerLoadPct') lt 40 and get_value(resource, 'signals.p95CpuPct') lt 40

recommendationRules.recommendation: Move to the cheapest valid SKU/capacity that satisfies p99 memory, throughput, connection, persistence, and availability requirements with production buffer.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: p95UsedMemoryPct < 40 AND p95ServerLoadPct < 40 AND p95CpuPct < 40 AND p95OpsPerSecond low for 14-30 days

recommendationRules.recommendation: Scale up capacity, tune maxmemory policy, add TTL hygiene, or reduce cache footprint.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.p95ServerLoadPct') gte 80 or get_value(resource, 'signals.p95CpuPct') gte 80 or get_value(resource, 'signals.p95LatencyMicroseconds') gte 5000

recommendationRules.recommendation: Scale up, scale out with clustering, reduce expensive commands, optimize key patterns, or move to Enterprise tier where appropriate.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: p95ServerLoadPct > 80 OR p95CpuPct > 80 OR p95LatencyMicroseconds exceeds SLO

recommendationRules.recommendation: Review TTLs, key design, cache warming, item size, memory pressure, and application cache-aside behavior before scaling.
Increase capacity or change memory policy only after validating whether eviction is acceptable for the workload.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: evictionsPerHour > 0 for production cache where evictions are not expected

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use connection pooling, reuse clients, reduce short-lived connections, and scale if connection limits remain constrained.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: connectionChurnPerSecond exceeds threshold OR connected clients approach SKU limit

recommendationRules.recommendation: Review key distribution, hash tags, cluster shard count, and hot-key patterns.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.idleDays') gte 30 and get_value(resource, 'signals.productionCritical') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Delete, downsize, or schedule decommission after owner validation.

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.persistenceRequired') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.persistenceEnabled') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Enable persistence or move to a tier that supports required data durability.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: workload requires recovery beyond cache rebuild and persistence is disabled or tier does not support required persistence

condition.pythonExpression: get_value(resource, 'signals.productionCritical') eq True and get_value(resource, 'signals.haMeetsPolicy') neq True

recommendationRules.recommendation: Use replicas, zone redundancy, clustering, geo-replication, or Enterprise tier based on availability requirements.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: productionCritical == true AND HA/zone/replica posture does not satisfy RTO/RPO

recommendationRules.recommendation: Disable non-SSL port and require TLS clients.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: enableNonSslPort == true OR non-SSL metric dimension shows usage

recommendationRules.recommendation: Raise minimum TLS version after validating client compatibility.
Use private endpoints or VNet integration where supported, disable public access where possible, and narrow firewall rules.
recommendationRules.conditionDescription: publicNetworkAccess enabled and policy requires private access

selectionAlgorithm: Discover metric definitions if cache is cold.
Fetch all required Redis metrics in one metrics call per cache.
Calculate memory, server load, CPU, hit rate, eviction, connection, latency, bandwidth, and shard-skew signals.
Fetch actual cost from Cost Management grouped by ResourceId and meter.
Fetch retail prices for candidate Redis SKUs and regions.
Build candidate targets that preserve memory, throughput, persistence, clustering, HA, TLS, and networking requirements.
Remove candidates that fail production filters.
Score remaining candidates using configured weights.
Return the highest-scoring recommendation with reason, estimated impact, risk, and validation requirements.

bestRecommendation.action: scale_down | scale_up | scale_out | tune | secure | delete | no_action

knownLimitations: Redis application-level key patterns, item sizes, command mix, and TTL behavior may require application telemetry or diagnostic logs beyond platform metrics.
Retail prices are public prices and may not reflect negotiated rates, commitments, reservations, or enterprise discounts; actual cost should come from Cost Management.
Scaling, clustering, persistence, TLS, and private access changes can affect client compatibility, failover behavior, data loss tolerance, and latency.
Low hit rate is not always bad; some Redis workloads use Redis for pub/sub, rate limiting, queues, or ephemeral state where cache hit rate is not the primary signal.
Azure Cache for Redis and Redis Enterprise have different feature, pricing, scaling, and persistence models; target selection must preserve required features.

conditionLanguage.fieldPathRoot: normalized DB resource record passed to analyzer
conditionLanguage.unknownHandling: return investigate or cap classification; never mark stay/best from missing required data

classificationLevels.meaning: Already optimized or only minor optional improvements remain.
Generally healthy with low-risk optimization opportunities.
Needs review; measurable cost, performance, reliability, security, or governance risk exists.
Action recommended; resource is materially over-costed, underperforming, risky, or non-compliant.
Immediate action recommended; high waste, outage risk, security exposure, or compliance risk.

evaluationOrder: apply best conditions only when no warning or worst condition matched
apply scoring caps for missing data, security findings, reliability findings, and unknown ownership
return score, classification, matchedConditions, and deterministicRecommendations
notes: Do not parse recommendationRules.condition at runtime; those are descriptive only.
Python assessment must use pythonAssessment.deterministicCases plus normalized metrics/signals.
All runtime decisions must be reproducible from input JSON and this file.

Store normalized values in the database using the roots resource, properties, metrics, cost, prices, tags, policy, advisor, and signals.

Evaluate rule condition groups with supported operators only.
Return matched outputs including recommendationAction/actionOutcome, message, severity, pillar, resultCode, and evidence.

analyzerContract.purpose: FastAPI/backend analyzer loads this JSON as deterministic optimization rules and evaluates them against metrics/properties/cost already stored in the database.

normalizedInputRecord.resource: identity and inventory fields such as id, name, type, subscriptionId, resourceGroup, location, sku, kind, tags, and lifecycle timestamps
normalizedInputRecord.properties: latest ARM/Resource Graph properties stored by collector
normalizedInputRecord.metrics: Azure Monitor metric aggregates stored by collector; expected aggregate suffixes include Avg, Min, Max, P50, P90, P95, P99, Count, Total, TrendPct, and WindowDays where available
normalizedInputRecord.cost: Cost Management actual/amortized/forecast rows normalized by resourceId, meter, skuName, product, chargeType, pricingModel, reservation/savings-plan coverage, and period totals
normalizedInputRecord.prices: optional Retail Prices/API or internal price cache for candidate comparison
normalizedInputRecord.policy: environment, production flag, criticality, required baseline, business hours, retention, and compliance requirements
normalizedInputRecord.signals: derived booleans/numbers calculated by collector/analyzer from DB metrics, properties, cost, and policy

conditionEvaluation.numericComparisonBehavior: compare only when stored value is numeric; otherwise rule_not_applicable
conditionEvaluation.stringComparisonBehavior: case_insensitive for contains/in/not_in when analyzer normalizes strings
conditionEvaluation.arrayComparisonBehavior: contains checks scalar membership or substring membership depending on stored value type
conditionEvaluation.booleanComparisonBehavior: accept true/false booleans; analyzer may normalize string booleans before evaluation

evaluationOrder: load resource-specific assessment file by resourceType
read one normalized resource record from DB with resource/properties/metrics/cost/tags/policy/signals/prices/advisor roots
evaluate data completeness and missing-cost rules
evaluate stop_or_delete rules before resize rules
evaluate upgrade rules before downgrade when both saturation and low-utilization signals conflict
evaluate stay rules only when no higher-priority action matched
return matched rule outputs with recommendationAction/actionOutcome and evidence paths

outputContract.actionFieldMeaning: normalized machine action outcome, one of stay/downgrade/upgrade/stop_or_delete/investigate
